operating_state: Total revenue declined 9% to $19.3 billion driven by automotive sales decreasing 21% from simultaneous factory changeovers to New Model Y production, resulting in approximately 51,000 fewer deliveries and lower average selling prices from sales mix and customer incentives. Energy generation and storage revenue surged 67% from increased Megapack and Powerwall deployments. Services revenue grew 15% from higher Supercharging, insurance, and used vehicle sales.
cost_structure: Total gross margin compressed from 17.4% to 16.3% as automotive gross margin fell to 16.2% due to lower average selling prices partially offset by reduced raw material costs and regulatory credit increases. Energy storage gross margin expanded to 28.8% driven by higher-margin product mix and lower per-unit costs. Research and development expenses increased 22% primarily from AI program investments, while SG&A expenses decreased 9% from reduced employee and facilities costs.
material_events: Completed industry-first simultaneous production line changeover across all factories for New Model Y launch, demonstrating operational capabilities despite several weeks of lost production. Deployed 10.4 GWh of energy storage products through Q1 2025. Received $101 million in proceeds from directors in shareholder settlement net of legal fees.
macro_exposures: Recently announced import tariffs expected to significantly impact battery cell expenses and energy storage business more than automotive operations. Retaliatory tariffs and export controls pose risks to global supply chain cost structure and component availability. Rising interest rates continue affecting vehicle financing affordability, while inflationary pressures and currency fluctuations create ongoing demand and margin pressures.
forward_outlook: Management expects capital expenditures to exceed $10 billion in 2025 for AI infrastructure, factory expansion, and new product development, though trade policy changes may necessitate project timeline adjustments. Focus on launching new affordable models later in 2025 utilizing next-generation platform and advancing autonomous capabilities through Cybercab development. Continued emphasis on cost reduction, vertical integration, and supply chain localization.
uncertainty_profile: Production ramp uncertainties for Cybertruck, Tesla Semi, and next-generation platform vehicles amid concurrent international projects and potential component constraints. Autonomous vehicle regulatory approval timeline remains unclear for Robotaxi deployment. Bitcoin holdings subject to mark-to-market volatility with $125 million loss recorded. Foreign currency exposure on significant intercompany balances creates ongoing earnings volatility without typical hedging protection.
